title: rubus tereticaulis subsp. leptopus
intro: Rubus tereticaulis subsp. leptopus, also known as the Japanese bramble or Japanese raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus tereticaulis plant, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and characterized by its thorny stems and edible fruits. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Rubus tereticaulis subsp. leptopus include reducing inflammation and pain, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive health. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
